title: resolve_path rejects .. components but still susceptible to symlink escape and absolute path escape
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access (Path Traversal)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/file/mod.rs lines 79-98 (resolve_path); used by execute_read_file (file/mod.rs:108), execute_write_file (file/write.rs:14), execute_edit_file (file/edit.rs:14), execute_list_files (file/list.rs:10), execute_notebook_edit (file/notebook.rs:36).

**Violation:**
resolve_path only blocks the literal '..' Component in the path string. It has THREE independent bypasses:
1. **Absolute-path escape:** if p.is_absolute() the function returns it as-is (line 81), so a model can read_file '/etc/passwd', '/root/.ssh/id_rsa', '/home/other_user/.bash_history'. The supposed 'path traversal' protection is toothless because the attacker does not need '..' when absolute paths are accepted. There is no confinement to a project root.
2. **Symlink escape for reads:** execute_read_file (mod.rs:108-111) calls resolve_path but NEVER canonicalizes; fs::read_to_string (read.rs:289) follows symlinks. A write to /tmp/alias -> /etc/shadow earlier in the session (or a pre-existing symlink) lets the attacker read anywhere fs::read_to_string can reach. Contrast execute_write_file (write.rs:20) and execute_edit_file (edit.rs:20) which DO canonicalize — the read path is the outlier.
3. **TOCTOU between canonicalize and fs::write:** write.rs lines 20-34 canonicalize, then line 62 does fs::write(path, ...). A symlink swap between those two calls resolves to a different target. Write is not open-with-O_NOFOLLOW.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Introduce a project-root jail: resolve_path must take a root: &Path (the session cwd at startup, stored in SessionState) and reject any resolved path whose canonicalized form is not a prefix-descendant of that root. All four call sites receive the root from ToolContext.
2. Canonicalize BEFORE the read path (read_text_file, read_image_file, read_pdf_file, read_notebook_file) and verify containment.
3. Use OpenOptions with custom_flags(libc::O_NOFOLLOW) on Unix (and equivalent on Windows via symlink_metadata pre-check) for write_file and edit_file to close the TOCTOU window.
4. Block absolute paths outside the jail even when the caller explicitly provides them — this is the model speaking, not the user. If the user needs out-of-root access, they must configure an allowlist (crate::permissions::FileAccessPolicy).
5. Delete the toothless 'Path traversal not allowed' check at lines 89-95 or keep it as defense-in-depth BUT document that it is not the primary defense.
